title: Corruption vs Stability
subtitle: The scandal is real. So is the fear of what comes next.

turns.prompt: Leaked documents tie senior officials to kickbacks on major contracts. Markets wobble. Allies urge caution.

choices.label: Launch a fully independent public inquiry

delayed.headline: Inquiry revelations keep institutions in crisis

choices.reaction: Headlines explode. Investors wait for names.

choices.label: Handle it internally with a quiet internal review
Sacrifice implicated ministers immediately
choices.reaction: Resignations dominate news. Questions remain about who knew.

turns.prompt: Investigations widen. Foreign partners ask whether contracts will be honoured.

choices.label: Freeze suspicious contracts pending review
choices.reaction: Infrastructure stalls. Clean vendors wait in line.

choices.label: Offer immunity for testimony against ringleaders
choices.reaction: Witnesses step forward. Public outrage mixes with relief.

choices.label: Reassure markets — business as usual
choices.reaction: Stock indices recover. Street vendors sell scandal papers — and umbrellas.

turns.prompt: Prosecutors circle your party. Civil servants threaten strikes. The opposition demands early elections.

choices.label: Push electoral and party-finance reform
choices.flavour: Change the rules that enabled this.
choices.reaction: Reform bills crawl through committee — three party donors quietly retire mid-debate.

choices.label: Appoint loyalists to key judicial posts
choices.flavour: Win the cases before they start.
choices.reaction: Cases slow down. The constitutional law department orders new textbooks — backdated by a decade.

choices.label: Call an early election to reset legitimacy
choices.flavour: Ask voters to absolve or expel you.

turns.prompt: A major ally freezes aid. Domestic banks restrict lending. Corruption and stability can no longer coexist.

memory_callbacks.text: The cover-up you chose is now the bigger scandal.
Markets trusted your calm — voters did not.
The inquiry you opened keeps finding names you hoped would stay buried.

choices.label: Prosecute everyone implicated, regardless of rank
choices.reaction: Handcuffs on evening news. Three ministries forget how to sign off a budget.

choices.label: Negotiate a stability pact — reforms without prosecutions
Hand power to a technocratic caretaker government
choices.reaction: Career civil servants take the wheel. Elections wait.

turns.prompt: Final reckoning. What kind of institutions did you leave?

choices.label: Leave cleaner institutions, however painful the path
choices.reaction: Handshakes get rarer in contract rooms. Power learns new manners — slowly.

choices.label: Preserve stability at the cost of deep reform
choices.reaction: Deals flow again. Young professionals plan their exit — quietly, on LinkedIn.

choices.label: Enshrine permanent citizen oversight of public money
choices.reaction: Budgets go live online by Tuesday. By Friday, a student-built dashboard has more readers than the official one.
